aliases.description: All of these terms mean the same sandwich format.
Panini and ciabatta are interchangeable terms for the same pressed bread format.
Lunch size and small dish are the same.
Dinner size and large dish are the same.
Common ways customers refer to Pepperjack cheese.
Common ways customers refer to mayonnaise (including common misspellings).
Common word/misspellings indicating an order is being placed; does not select roll/sub/wrap.
Common ways customers request French Fries.

format_conflicts.description: Only one format group may be selected per item (e.g., bread vs dish).
format_conflicts.conflict_rule: If more than one format group is selected for a single item, require clarification.
format_conflicts.clarification_prompt: Just to confirm — did you want that as a sub (hoagie/hero/wedge) or as a small dish (lunch size)?

definitions.definition: The sandwich or wrap is heated on a flat grill (panini press) until the bread is toasted and the cheese is melted (if applicable). This adds a nice crunch.
Bread that is heated and toasted via pressing. Interchangeable with ciabatta for ordering.
Panini-style bread that is heated and toasted via pressing. Interchangeable to order with panini.
Roughly similar to a roll-size portion.
Roughly similar to a sub-size portion.
Interchangeable with 'Small dish'. They are the same.
Interchangeable with 'Large dish'. They are the same.

clarification_triggers.ask: Sure — would you like that on a roll, sub, wrap, panini, small dish, or large dish?
What type of roll would you like?
What type of wrap would you like?
What type of sub would you like?
How would you like your eggs cooked?

pricing_rule.notes: Double meat cost equals 2x the base meat price for the current format.
When two meats are selected in half_half mode, price equals the base meat price of the higher-priced meat for the current format.
